biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — 11/12 tool-using units (92%; units 4,6,8,21,25,32,34,35,40,45,46); ls-via-Bash head/tail split (unit 4), grep-rln-via-Bash and rg-l-via-Bash x2 and rg-via-Bash x5+ and cat|grep pipeline on package.json (unit 4), grep-n-via-Bash and rg-via-Bash as section anchors and wc-l-via-Bash (unit 6), rg-via-Bash x4+ as section anchors between Edit calls (unit 8), rg-l-via-Bash and for-loop-rg-via-Bash (unit 21), ls-via-Bash (unit 25), find-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x6+ (unit 32), cat-n-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x4+ and for-loop-Bash success=false and rm-via-Bash success=false (unit 34), /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x3+ (unit 35), /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x1 (unit 40), grep-rn-via-Bash and grep-rln-via-Bash x2 and for-loop-Bash success=false (unit 45), /usr/bin/grep x5+ and xargs-grep-pipeline success=false (unit 46); rate 92% matches prior cycle peak exactly; zero internalization across 31+ consecutive measured cycles; structurally identical to all prior cycles with no change in violation type or frequency

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — units 6,8,32,34; unit 6: 8+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg-via-Bash section anchors and Reads; unit 8: Edit(success=false) then rg-via-Bash anchor then Read then 7+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg anchors; unit 32: 3 Edit calls on data-history-v3.tsx plus 6 Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx plus 3 Edit calls on s1-upload.tsx; unit 34: 2 Edit calls on data-history-row-actions.tsx plus 3 Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx; peak Edit count per file and distribution consistent with all prior measured cycles; document-editing thrash on plan.md co-occurs with shell_tool_preference_violations as section-locating strategy in units 6 and 8 confirming established causal link; no improvement trend across 14+ consecutive cycles

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:STABLE — units 4 and 46; unit 4: Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false) before ls-via-Bash pivot to correct path; unit 46: Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false) before ls-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep pivot then correct path src/utils/hooks/use-query-sync/index.ts read successfully; prior cycle (2026-05-08) reclassified from IMPROVING back to WORSENING after pattern reverted to double-instance; this cycle confirms double-instance is now the stable baseline not a transient reversion; cross-session internalization remains zero across 20+ consecutive sessions; agent can locate the correct path after failure but consistently defaults to wrong path at turn start

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg-via-Bash anchor plus Read plus successful Edit; unit 34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before /usr/bin/grep anchor plus successful Edit; two instances consistent with all prior thirteen measured cycles at 1-2 instances per cycle; no improvement trend across 14+ consecutive cycles

rm_instead_of_trash:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 34: Bash rm -f /tmp/fs-tests/count-searches.ts (success=false, blocked by hook) followed by correct trash recovery; reappeared after absence in one prior cycle which is now confirmed anomalous; self-correction within same unit confirmed; initial violation reflex uncorrected despite repeated hook blocks across multiple sessions; CLAUDE.md rule states trash not rm unconditionally
cross_references: dream insight 'Never assert state without observation' violated by optimistic_edit_without_read in units 8 and 34 — Edit attempted without reading current file state producing success=false; also violated by path_assumption_bias in units 4 and 46 where a file path is asserted as known without verification; insight remains unimplemented at behavioral level across this and prior cycles

dream insight 'context reconstruction after session boundaries is a data-hallucination risk' confirmed by path_assumption_bias — agent reconstructs use-sync-q.ts as the canonical hook path from stale prior-session state when the actual location is src/utils/hooks/use-query-sync/index.ts; reconstructed path is presented as known fact until Read failure forces correction; this is exactly the hallucinated-reconstruction failure mode the insight describes

dream insight 'values connecting to external systems must be read from canonical source before use' violated by path_assumption_bias — use-sync-q.ts is treated as the canonical hook location without probing; the agent does not Glob or Grep for the actual location before attempting the Read, relying instead on inferred path from prior session context

dream insight 'fix-thrash loops correlate with context limit hits' not directly triggered in this session — unit 35 shows extensive iterative fiber-snatcher exploration with multiple approach pivots (heredoc escaping failures, python inline parsing failures) but these appear to be genuine API surface exploration with each pivot producing new information rather than a thrash loop caused by lost root-cause diagnosis across a context boundary
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations has shown zero internalization across 31+ consecutive cycles — repeated flagging has no effect; structural intervention at the harness layer is required; a PreToolUse hook blocking Bash calls matching patterns (grep|/usr/bin/grep|find.*-name|ls.*\||cat.*\|) with a redirect message naming the appropriate dedicated tool (Grep/Glob/Read) would enforce compliance at the tool-call layer rather than relying on post-hoc awareness; deprioritize further recommendation text on this bias until a hook is evaluated

path_assumption_bias on use-sync-q.ts is now confirmed stable at double-instance per session across multiple cycles after the IMPROVING trend failed to hold; the agent consistently defaults to the stale path despite correcting it within session; adding an explicit canonical-path note to project CLAUDE.md (src/utils/hooks/use-query-sync/index.ts) or a session-start memory probe surfacing the correct location would address the root cause more durably than repeated flagging

re_edit_thrash causal link to shell_tool_preference_violations is structurally confirmed (rg-via-Bash used as section locator between Edit calls on plan.md); eliminating the rg-via-Bash anchor step by using the Grep tool for section location would directly reduce Edit call count; this is a downstream fix of the shell_tool_preference_violations intervention rather than a separate action

optimistic_edit_without_read has persisted at 1-2 instances per cycle for 14+ consecutive cycles with no trend; a PreToolUse hook checking whether the Edit target was read in the current session before allowing the Edit call would enforce the read-before-edit invariant at the harness layer; current flagging alone is insufficient
